data_minimization.reasoning: The policy does not mention collection of biometric data, so no data minimization assessment can be made for this category.

collected.policy_snippet: Food and Activity Diary Data may include sensitive personal information when the information indicates or allows someone to infer a health condition.
collected.purpose: Health data is collected as part of Food and Activity Diary Data to support the provision of the Services, including tracking dietary habits, fitness goals, body measurements, physiological conditions, and lifestyle information.

data_minimization.reasoning: The policy describes a broad range of health-related data collected, including dietary habits, medications, calorie counts, dietary restrictions, body measurements, physiological conditions, sleeping data, and progress photos. No concrete limits are specified on what health data is strictly necessary, and the collection scope appears extensive without clear justification for each data type.

stored.policy_snippet: We will retain your personal information as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes outlined in this Policy (including to satisfy our legal or reporting requirements), unless a longer retention period is required or allowed under law.
stored.purpose: Health data, as part of personal information, is retained to fulfill the purposes described in the policy, including service provision and legal obligations, but no specific retention period for health data is defined.

shared.policy_snippet: We may disclose your personal information to our research partners, including those that perform surveys or research projects in partnership with us or on our behalf.
shared.purpose: Health data, which forms part of personal information including Food and Activity Diary Data, is shared with service providers, business partners, research partners, marketing and advertising partners, analytics providers, and law enforcement as described in the policy.

health_data.overall_privacy_risk_description: MyFitnessPal collects extensive health-related data including medications, physiological conditions, body measurements, and dietary habits, which may reveal sensitive health conditions. This data is shared broadly with service providers, research partners, marketing and advertising partners, and analytics providers, and may be transferred internationally to countries with different privacy protections. Retention is vague, with no specific deletion timeline for health data, posing significant risks of prolonged exposure of sensitive health information.

physical_data.overall_privacy_risk_description: MyFitnessPal collects sensitive physical data including body measurements, progress photos, and location information, and shares this broadly with service providers, marketing partners, and analytics providers. collected.policy_snippet: MyFitnessPal may collect Usage and Device Data. This may include details about your browser, operating system, or device (such as your cookie identifiers, internet protocol (IP) address, ​​mobile advertising identifier, and other unique identifiers), internet service provider, mobile carrier, general location information (including inferred location based off of your IP address), actions that you take while using the Services, information about how you interact with the Services, including the frequency and duration of your activities, information about the links you click, and other information about how you use the Services.
collected.purpose: Behavioral data such as usage patterns, interaction frequency, link clicks, and activity duration are collected to enhance user experience, ensure security, prevent fraud, provide personalized content and advertisements, and analyze service usage.

data_minimization.reasoning: The policy describes broad collection of behavioral data including actions taken, frequency and duration of activities, links clicked, browsing patterns, cross-device tracking, and targeted advertising data. No concrete limits are specified on what behavioral data is collected, and the policy uses permissive language ('may collect') without meaningful restrictions or justifications for each data element.

stored.policy_snippet: We will retain your personal information as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes outlined in this Policy (including to satisfy our legal or reporting requirements), unless a longer retention period is required or allowed under law.
stored.purpose: Behavioral data is retained as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes described in the policy, including service provision, legal obligations, and reporting requirements, without specifying a defined retention period.

retention_policy.policy_snippet_deletion: We will retain your personal information as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes outlined in this Policy (including to satisfy our legal or reporting requirements), unless a longer retention period is required or allowed under law.

shared.policy_snippet: We may disclose your personal information, including device and browser information and activity and usage information, to our marketing and advertising partners, including social media platforms, third-party advertising networks, and other parties that assist us in serving, measuring the performance of, and optimizing our advertising campaigns.
shared.purpose: Behavioral data including device, browser, and activity/usage information is shared with marketing and advertising partners, analytics providers, and research partners to serve targeted ads, measure ad performance, and conduct analytics.

behavioral_data.overall_privacy_risk_description: MyFitnessPal collects extensive behavioral data including usage patterns, interaction frequency, link clicks, and cross-device tracking, which is shared broadly with marketing, advertising, and analytics partners. The retention period is vague ('as long as necessary') with no clear deletion timeline, and data is explicitly transferred internationally to countries with potentially weaker privacy protections. The combination of broad behavioral data collection, wide third-party sharing for targeted advertising, and cross-device tracking poses significant privacy risks to users.
